relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The Philistine coalition gathers while Israel waits near Jezreel.
Military units pass in order, with David's exile force positioned behind Achish.
Other commanders challenge the presence of Hebrews whom Achish trusts.
They refuse to risk a battlefield reversal and imagine David regaining Saul's favor through their deaths.
The women's victory song remains part of David's international reputation.
Achish invokes YHWH and his own experience of David, yet coalition authority overrules him.
He dismisses David peacefully rather than forcing him into divided-loyalty combat.
David maintains the role of loyal servant and asks why he is excluded.
Achish praises him extravagantly but acknowledges the commanders' decision.
He orders David's group to depart at first light.
The refugee force returns south while Philistine armies continue toward Jezreel.

contemplative_note: David is released from a battle whose loyalties would have torn every available identity. The contemplative task is to honor boundaries without converting wartime suspicion into permanent ethnic distrust.
